rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Strengthening Psychosocial Disability Rights
rows.description: Persons with psychosocial disability face daily barriers due to inhumane treatment, discrimination and exclusion in society which is further exacerbated by lack of strong disability rights and protection laws in Nepal. There is a need to identify persons with psychosocial disability and support them in their recovery, build a strong network among them as well as empower them about their rights, form a coalition with underrepresented disability groups and encourage them to demand for their rights with the duty bearers at their local and province level. Moreover, there is a need to sensitize families and communities regarding psychosocial disability and support in their social inclusion and improving livelihood. This project aims to respond to these needs and the results of project activities are: MPs from the Federal and Province Parliament take measures to formulate psychosocial disability friendly laws or amend laws which are discriminatory / derogatory towards persons with psychosocial disability; bureaucrats from the federal, province and local level take measures to include psychosocial disability in the plans and programs which are developed; and persons with psychosocial disabilities and coalition of underrepresented groups are engaged in evidence based self-advocacy to mainstream psychosocial disability in Sudurpaschhim Province and Karnali Province. The project beneficiaries are 670  persons with disabilities (including psychosocial disability) and their family members, members of National Federation of Disabled Nepal (NFDN), members of community groups (womens groups, youth groups, agriculture groups, religious groups, consumer groups) and journalists; and 230 government officlas and law-makers in the local, provincial and federal level. The project is implemented by Koshish - National Mental Health Self-Help Organization (https://www.koshishnepal.org/).

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Enhancing Well-being of Migrant Workers
rows.description: Migration for foreign employment has become a major source of income for the Nepalese. Though this has brought economic profits to many Nepali households and the nation, it has come with a costs. Those are the psychological stress and mental health problems, resulting in family disintegration,emotional and behavioral problems of children. Studies on living and working conditions of low-skilled migrants consistently revealed abuse and exploitation, including forced labor and trafficking. These ar reflected also on the migrants' family members during the absence but above all during the home living periods of the migrant workers.  The trauma experinced by the returning migrant workers, or their families, are not spoken of, resulting in behavioral problems, reduced efficiency, loosing comprehension and cognitive ability, withdrawal etc. These have a ngatve impact on person's human relationships, wellbeing, health and and productivity. As a result of project, migrant workers and their families have improved their mental health and psychosocial wellbeing. Returnee migrant worker and their families have been engaged in self-employment, business or other livelihood initiatives. Also, local government has replicated the best practices of the project with sustained allocation or resources. The project beneficiaries are the 2 560 migrant workers and their families, and 1 200 government officials and members of the organizations of persons with disabilities (DPOs) in Kailali and Salyan districts.  The project is implemented by Centre for Mental Health and Counseling Nepal (CMC-Nepal) (https://www.cmcnepal.org.np/).

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Climate Resilient Villages
rows.description: Project areas of Kalikot and Jajarkot districts in Nepal face several climate and livelihood challenges, made even worse by the Covid-19 that led the return of more than 50,000 migrant workers from India with no alternative income sources. These are contributing to already low agriculture productivity particularly among worse hit poor and vulnerable families such as women-headed households, Dalit and persons with disabilities. The aim of this project is to improve women-friendly, environmentally sustainable, and climate-resilient livelihood of these vulnerable and marginalized communities. Results of project activities: i) increased capacity in planning, management and implementation of women-friendly climate resilient and disaster risk reduction plans, ii) increased food production, nutrition and income through climate resilient, ecological agriculture and green market enterprises, and iii) improved capacity in implementing climate resilient and disaster risk reduction plans and programmes. The project beneficiaries are socially disadvantaged and marginalized farming families, particularly Dalits families, women, adolescent girls and persons with disabilities. Project activities also benefit CBNOs, farmer cooperatives, farmers? groups, SAMVAD centers, Eco Clubs, child right clubs, students, teachers and duty bearer bodies such as Palika offices, including Mayors and other elected members. The project is implemented by Group of Helping Hands (SAHAS) Nepal (https://www.sahasnepal.org.np/).

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Strengthening FarmersŽ Livelihood
rows.description: The causes of poverty in Nepal, one of the poorest countries in the world, are complex and multidimensional. One of the key factors are the problems faced in the agriculture sector. The agriculture sector employs 65% of the population and contributes 24% of the national GDP. However, productivity of in the sector is low and smallholder families are rarely able to earn sufficient living. The Sudurpaschim Province is one of the least developed provinces in Nepal, characterized by marginality, inaccessibility, fragility, and a high rate of poverty. There is also a high rate of male migration, especially youths, in search of overseas employment, leading to increased feminization of agriculture. Despite the potential for agriculture, the province suffers from net food deficit. More than a third of its population (33.6%) is considered multidimensionally poor (compared to nationwide 28,6%). Child mortality and malnutrition rate are high, as are anaemia and stunting. Less than half of the children and women have a sufficiently diversified diet. The project aims at realization of the right to food and livelihood for approximately 21 000 persons of the target communities, as well as their improved climate change adaptation and disaster risk management. Project will organize and capacitate beneficiaries, provide them skills, resources and collaborate with approximately 1 400 local government staff and other key stakeholders to achieve this.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: School Mental Health Program
rows.description: Mental health problems are still not adequately identified, prevented, and treated in Nepal. Especially school-going children of ultra-poor families and children with disabilities are at risk of developing mental health and psychosocial problems. The recent study (2020) carried out by the Nepal Health Research Council has revealed that the mental health problems are found among 5.2% children of the age group from 13 to 17 years, whereas these problems increased to 10% above 18 years. Due to lack of awareness about emotional and behavioral problems (EBPs) of children, teachers and parents practice corporal punishment. Student counselling is not avalable at school. There is a serious problem in student drop out and EBPs. The impact of the project is that the school-going children and adolescent are able to develop to their full potential, and their learning result improve as their psychosocial wellbeing grows stronger.Teachers have increased capacity to practice child friendly classroom management and parenting education. Parent and other stakeholders have increased their support in promotion of psychosocial wellbeing of children. Child protection policy and practice are in place in project schools. School based psycho-social support unit is functioning in program schools. Selfhelp Groups protect the rights of the persons with mental health and psycho-social disabilities. By 2025, master trainers are developed at national, province and local level to replicate comprehensive school counseling. CMC-Nepal has increased collaboration with the Government of Nepal to formulate and endorse comprehensive school counselling policy.The project beneficiaries are school going children and adolescents, with especial focus on children with emotional and behavioral problems, other students in the project area, adults with mental health and psychosocial problems, and parents, families, and care takers of these people. The project is implemented by Centre for Mental Health and Counseling Nepal (CMC-Nepal) (https://www.cmcnepal.org.np/).

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: F.a: Multilingual Education Program
rows.description: Soru and Mugum Karmarong, the remotest rural municipalities of Mugu district in Nepal, lack the access to inclusive quality education. There is a lack of inclusive environment and no equitable access to school education for children from poor and marginalised families including ethnic minorities and children with disabilities (CwDs). The aim of the project is to increase equitable education opportunities and access to quality inclusive education for all children, especially for the poor and marginalised including children with disabilities and ethnic/language minority in the project areas. Results of project activities: i) the targeted children, especially girls, language minorities and CwDs from project area have equal access to inclusive quality school education, ii) schools and parents cooperate to increase the inclusive education opportunities for poor, marginalised, ethnic, and other minority groups, and iii) rural municipalities are responsive towards inclusive quality education and development of disabilities and language minority groups.  The project beneficiaries include school children including ethnic minority Karmarong children and dropped out children, children and persons with disabilities, and  School Management Committee (SMC) / Parent Teacher Association (PTA) members, teachers and parents   in Soru RM and Mugun Karmarong RM. The project is implemented by United Mission to Nepal.

rows.description: The project will promote clean cooking solutions in Nepal - contributing to reduction of green house gas emission and improvement of the health condition of women in rural areas. rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Frame agreement with NGO (Fida Int.)
rows.description:
Empowering Partners for Social Responsibility. The project aims to increase the capacity of a local partner to do holistic development work in local communities. Nepal is one of the poorest countries in the world, where majority of the people live in rural
 areas. Fida?s partner in Nepal is the Assemblies of God church (AOG). The project provides training and awareness-raising for the partner on a great variety of issues related to development work. Fida and AOG work together in the local communities and thr
ough this hands-on development work the partner acquires practical skills. The project focuses on three major components: Literacy, Community Health Education (CHE) and HIV/AIDS. As AOG?s skills and knowledge grow, the partner will become increasingly capa
ble of doing effective community development work of its own. This will contribute to the increasing impact of the partner?s work at community level.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Frame agreement with NGO (Fida Int.)
rows.description:
Maithili Empowerment Programme. The programme seeks to increase a local partner?s capacity to respond to the needs of the Maithili minority in Southern Nepal. The 2.5 million Maithili of Southern Nepal make up the country?s largest ethnic minority. Formerl
y known for their rich culture and traditions, the Maithili now live in poverty and have almost lost their unique identity. To address the needs of the Maithili, Fida partners with a local NGO called Relative Nepal, which belongs to the Assemblies of God N
epal (AGN) church. The programme offers the partner hands-on training in community development work. The main approach used is that of Community Health Education. The work carried out among the Maithili communities includes e.g. literacy training, basic ed
ucation and cultural revitalization activities. Through the trainings and practical development work conducted during the programme, the partner?s capacities build up and, at the same time, the needs of the Maithili communities are addressed. Already one s
chool has been established and children?s literacy classes started. These together will contribute towards the improvement of the quality of life in the Maithili communities.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Frame agreement with NGO (Fida Int.)
rows.description:
Youth Centre in Kathmandu. The project seeks to empower a local partner for youth work of its own. Now, after the civil war, the capital of Kathmandu is facing a huge wave of immigration from the rural areas to the city. The young generation in particular 
is moving into the city in search for work, but lacks sufficient skills and training. Fida partners with the Assemblies of God church (AOG) to address the needs of the youth. Fida and its partner will establish a youth centre in the capital that provides v
ocational training and counselling to the youth, and facilities for leisure time activities. The AOG church youth will act as volunteers at the centre. Through the youth centre the partner will learn to work with the youth in practice. The youth, in turn, 
will have their educational and other needs addressed. All in all, the project will improve the life skills of the youth.

rows.title: NGO Support / Promoting Child Rights Through Educational Settings
rows.description:
The school enrolment ratio of children in Nepal is almost 87 percent, but still the drop out rate is also very high, almost 100 children enrolled in grade one only 22 percent appear the school leaving examination. Children experienced discrimination, have 
suffered violence on the name of discipline, are victims of political conflict and have suffered sexual abuse. The school curriculum is not children's right responsive and friendly. The project aims to promote and protect child rights in and through educat
ional settings and policies with the active involvement of parents, community and children themselves. The project promotes significantly schools and other educational sectors role on promoting children's rights at local and national level and to ensure ch
ild rights in schools benefits children directly.

ject purpose is to ensure pro-poor sustainable socio-economic and environmental development in Koshi basin based on the principles of Intergrated River Basin Management. The objective is to reduce vulnerability of people, biodiversity, and economic investm
ents in the Koshi River basin caused by climate change and human activities.

rows.title: NGO Support / Bilingual Literacy Project in Lhomi and Nepali
rows.description:
The objective of this project is to increase the number of functional literate children and adults in both Lhomi and Nepali and create enough credibility from the local community to develop projects in the area of health, education, IGAs and advocacy.This 
will be done through organizing literacy classes in close cooperation with local village committees.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Regional Solid Waste Management Project in Nepal
rows.description:
The purpose of the project is to establish a sustainable, regional solid waste management system in the area of Sunsari and Morang districts in Nepal. This is achieved through an institutionalised cooperation between the municipalities of the two districts
 providing services also to the surrounding VDCs.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Increased Capacity of Hydrometeorological Services ICI-project
rows.description:
Finnish Meteorological Institute and the Department of Hydrlogy and Meteorology under the Ministry of Environment have together proposed a ICI-project for Increased Capacity of Hydrometeorological Services in Nepal. The project aims to improve the capacity
 of DHM in hydrometeorological observations, services and international data sharing and thus improve the capability of the Government of Nepal to respond to the increased risks of natural disasters related to weather and climate.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: FNBEs Support Services for Education Sector Cooperation in Nepal
rows.description:
The main purpose is to provide support in planning, follow-up and analysis to the Ministry for Foreign Affairs in relation to the development of education sector (especially TEVT) in Nepal. Part of the assignment is to assist in planning and follow-up of t
he implementation of the SSRP and to participate in the joint meetings and reviews.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Our school is a good school primary education development project
rows.description:
Nepal?s education system can?t offer child friendly school environment to all. Project?s development problem is that public schools and teachers lack adequate teaching learning methods and infrastructure resulting children dropping out of schools. This pro
ject aims to strengthen teacher?s role in their community and inside the education system, form learning network among teachers, promote child?s rights and participation in schools and sensitize stakeholders to take responsible role for implementation. Thr
ough these activities the objective of this project: Child Friendly School concept is widely accepted and practiced in public schools in the project area. Motivated and skilful teachers and inspiring and safe school environment are available to all the chi
ldren at the 14 schools in 5 VDCs will be reached.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Sanitation and Water for all in the context of Climate Change in Nepal
rows.description:
The UNICEF WASH-programme supports the Government of Nepal in its efforts to formulate and implement one national WASH program to achieve the national target of universal water supply and sanitation coverage  by 2017. The programme is implemented throug fi
ve components: 1)Support for capacity-building and formulation of a national WASH programme, 2) District level coordination and harmonisation and support for WASH programme interventions, 3) improved interagency coordination on WASH emergency preparedness 
and response, 4)Rollout of a comprehensive hygiene promotion initiative in selected diarrhoea-prone districts, 5): Scale-up of school WASH, hygiene promotion (including hand washing with soap) and community approaches to total sanitation (CATS), using chil
dren as agents of change.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Rural Village Water Resources Management Project, Second Phase
rows.description:
The overall objective of RVWRMP is institutionalised capacity at local and regional levels to sustain and continuously improve enhanced quality of life, better environmental conditions and increased opportunities in rural livelihoods in the project area. T
he purpose of the phase II is to achieve improved well-being and reduced poverty in project VDCs. The new phase emphasises the impacts of the project instead of means.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Support to National Human Rights Commission of Nepal
rows.description:
National Human Rights Commission, NHRC, is a central constitutional human rights body in Nepal. Commission?s work focuses on monitoring transitional justice and human rights promotion. Finland with other donors has been supporting a UNDP?s capacity buildin
g project. This project will further enhance the Commission?s capacity especially in the fields that intertwine with other Finnish involvement, such as, investigation of human rights violations and human rights work in education sector. The project duratio
n is 18 months during 2010-2012. The total budget is 185 000 euros.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Enhancing Child Protection System in Nepal
rows.description:
In spite of commitment, government of Nepal has been inefficient to meet the requirements for protecting children from different forms of abuse, violence and exploitation. NGOs have become crucial actors, though the actions of NGOs are also questioned and 
sometimes their capacities and skills are also questioned. The government child protection policies are still waiting for formal adaptation. The project aims at establishing child protection system and mechanism at local level with appropriate policy guida
nce. Specific objectives are: promotion of national child protection network, application of child protection policies, and strengthening of local child protection committees. The project has component of advocacy, network building, capacity building, and 
production and sharing of resource materials.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Integrating Sexual and Reproductive Health and Biodiversity
rows.description:
The project implements an integrated population, health and environment (PHE) approach. The objective is improved sustainable management of natural resources and increased access to sexual and reproductive health (SRH) services contributing to improved liv
elihood in environmentally critical areas of Terai Arc Landscape. Income generating activities, biodiversity conservation and SRH rights and services are promoted. Food security and climate change adaptation with some mitigation activities, as well as capa
cities of community-based organisations are developed.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Local Cooperation Fund (LCF) in Nepal
rows.description:
The overall objective of the project is to increase biodiesel feedstock and promote clean energy and rural income generation through management and cultivation of Jatropha curcas.Specific objectives are 1)to analyze the land use patterns and suitability fo
r Jatropha curcas cultivation, 2)to increase awareness on collection, management and cultivation of Jatropha curcas, 3)to produce Jatropha seedlings for commercial plantation,  4)to improve local economy through Jatropha seed production, 5)to establish mar
keting linkage between Jatropha producers and biodiesel company, 6)to conduct trial experiment for optimization of Jatropha genotype and place, 7)to conduct a pilot scale biodiesel testing program.

rows.title: Local Cooperation Fund (LCF) in Nepal
rows.description:

ensitize the non-dalit journalists and media houses on dalit issues to develop and mainstream dalit issues into their regular programme, Institutionalization of Radio Jagaran through internal system development and connecting with people and institutions u
nder cooperative model.

rows.title: UNWomen support for women in suffered in conflict in Nepal
rows.description:
UN Women will support coherence and coordination across the UN system in advancing women's participation in conflict resolution. UN Women will support existing and new efforts to improve the protection environment for women during and after conflict, to en
gage women in conflict prevention, and to ensure peacebuilding processes are guided by women's perspectives and address their needs. Promotion of equality, including gender equality, and women's and girl's rights are explicitly mentioned in the Finnish Dev
elopment Policy.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Environment administration strengthening in local level SEAM
rows.description:
The project aims to strengthen environmental administration and management at the local level in the industrial corridor of Nepal. By means of environmental advocacy and the project support local population is sensitized to actively pursue for imporovement
s  in their sanitary and hygienic conditions. The overall aim of the project is to introduce good practices for environment management systems and pollution control.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Improving the quality of education
rows.description:
The objective of the project is to improve the quality of education and to improve access to educational facilities of primary schools in Far Western Dadeldhura District, Nepal. At school and community level beneficiaries are trained and sensitised, and sc
hool facilities improved. At district level, education sector stakeholders are sensitised and trained, special focus is on the governmental bodies. Expeced results are improved enrolment and quality of education, and improved management of the educational 
bodies.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Strengthening rights and safety in forestry
rows.description:
The project aims at strengthening rights and safety of forest trade union members and individual wood workers, and improve the ecological values of the forests in Nepal by educating wood workers on sustainable forest management and biodiversity protection.
 Increased memberships of forest trade unions and safe working conditions is also an objective. Capacity of government agencies and trade unions to advocate for policies is increased to support sustainable forestry to address climate change.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Integrating mental health in primary health care
rows.description: Improving the quality of and the access to mental health services in Nepal's Dang district. Alleviating the stigma associated with mental illness.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Multi stakeholder forest sector support, transition phase
rows.description:
The Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC) acting through the Embassy of Switzerland has a lead role in the implementation of the Programme during 2011-2015. After the three year transition phase the programme is expected to be handed over to a
 national entity. The ultimate aim of the programme is to become a National Forestry Fund administered by the Government of Nepal. During the transition phase 22 districts will be covered. The programme area will eventually be extended to 61 districts in N
epal.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Frame agreement with NGO (Fida Int.)
rows.description:
Water and Sanitation Project WATSAN. The project purpose is the decreased incidence of intestinal deceases in rural communities in target VDCs in Kailali District, Far West, Nepal. Fida's partner, the New Life Service Association (NSA), will have project a
ctivities like awareness raising and capacity building of communities on health, sanitation and hygiene and water supply and toilet construction. It will use community mobilization and work in close coordination with partner agencies. The project will form
 Health and Sanitation groups (called WASH groups) in each VDC to address the root causes (contaminated water, poor sanitation and personal hygiene practices) of the main problem, high incidence of the intestinal diseases.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Local Cooperation Fund (LCF) in Nepal
rows.description:
To use Kathmandu International Mountain Film Festival (KIMFF) 2010 films, being a powerful medium, to advance better sensitivity and understanding among people on contemporary issues facing Nepali society with special focus on migrant labour, biodiversity,
 climate change and environment, cultural diversity, gender and development priorities. KIMFF hopes not only to encourage a deeper understanding of the socio-economic, cultural, and political realities of the country but also to create a sense that the sig
nificance of these issues is shared. The growing distance and complexity of the relationship between the centre and the districts is what KIMFF seeks to narrow down. The impetus for the festival has always been the trust of the community in the capability 
of the audio-visual medium in creating social and economic transformation.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Support to Constitution building in Nepal
rows.description:
"The project is to be implemented within the International IDEA ongoing project ""Supporting Constitution Building in Nepal"" by including additional outcomes and activities. The objectives are to 1) build capacity of key political actors involved in Nepal
's constitution making process and 2) initiate and deepen dialogue among key political actors towards building consensus on these constitutional issues."

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Joint evaluation of Support to Peace Building in Nepal
rows.description:
Joint evaluation takes place so that Denmark is the leader of the evaluation. Participants in the joint evaluation are those donors that have supported through different channels the peace building process in Nepal. Finland is one of these donors. It has b
een agreed with Denmark that Finland would mount a separate outreach study on the impact of the support to the various peace building and human rights funds on the village and community level and on individual people in particular women. This separate stud
y would be amalgamated in the actual evaluation as an input. The review would also contribute to our knowledge of Finland's suppor to the process of involving women in peace buildin.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: NEP/FLC HNFF Allo Enterprise for Sustainable Livelihoods
rows.description:
Overall objective of the project is to build an environmentally-sustainable value chain for allo from field to district-level centre for the benefit of 39 women allo producing groups in Sankhuwasabha Solukhumbu and Bhojpur Districts of Eastern Nepal.Specif
ic objectives are to ensure sustainable supply of raw material through cultivation and management of allo on private and common lands to adopt appropriate technologies for allo extraction and spinning and to build the capacity of newly registered   cottage
 industry for a sustainable allo enterprise.The main avtivities are: Training materials and co-funding support for allo cultivation Testing procurement and installation of spinning machine set that is adapted for allo fibres, establishing procedures for ef
ficient and equitable ownership and management, training local people on how to operate it Replication of environmentally-sound steaming techniques for fibre extraction to reduce fuel-wood usage and improve productivity Capacity-building of a self-supporti
ng producer-controlled community-based cottage industry to deliver services to producer groups.Expected results of the projec are: Increase in volume of allo raw material supply by 50% as compared with benchmark through environmentally-sustainable cultivat
ion and management of allo on private and common lands. Spinning machine set installed producing consistent quality allo yarn with corresponding reduction of unhealthy use of the drop spindle Energy-efficient extraction of allo fibre using a steaming metho
d Producer-controlled community-based cottage industry established with operational service centre.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Rural Water Supply and Sanitation Project-Western Nepal Completion Phase
rows.description:
RWSSP Completion Phase will continue support to advance rural water supply and sanitation in western Nepal. Nepal's national goal is to reach total clean water and sanitation coverage by 2017. Access to clean water and sanitation is one of the basic human 
rights. In this respect awareness of rural population on these rights will be increased and the capacity of the 'duty bearers' i.e. national institutions to respond with sufficient service provision will be strengthened. A phasing-out strategy will be prep
ared to ensure transfer of responsibilities and sustainability of results after the end of the project. Expansion of the project's scope to rural livelihoods will be explored during the planning phase. Arsenic mitigation will be taken into account in the T
erai-region.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Improving rehabilitation services for people with spinal cord injury in Western Nepal
rows.description:
Spinal cord injury rehabilitation is a major unmet need in Nepal. In Nepal most cases are caused by falls from heights chiefly from trees (from which poor farmers cut fodder for their stock) and building sites. Road traffic accidents are an increasing caus
e of SCI. There are currently only three centres in the whole country offering rehabilitation services to people with spinal cord injuries ? two of the three are in Kathmandu. GPHRC provides services to the Western Development Region and through four INF c
linics to the Mid-Western Development Region - a total population of around 8.5 million people. At present large proportion of SCI patients present for rehabilitation with pressure sores. These patients require admissions up to 6 months as the pressure sor
e needs to heal before rehabilitation can commence. Patients admitted without pressure sores can complete the rehabilitation within 6-8 weeks. The project ?Improving rehabilitation services for people with spinal cord injury in western Nepal? will be imple
mented by International Nepal Fellowship Nepal?s (INF/Nepal) Green Pastures Hospital and Rehabilitation Centre (GPHRC) in collaboration with government and private hospitals in Pokhara Nepal and INF?s Partnership for Rehabilitation (PFR) which supports com
munity-based rehabilitation services in the Western Region of Nepal. This project has three major objectives:1. To reduce the incidence of secondary complications in new cases of spinal cord injury (SCI) by providing health education and essential equipmen
t to patients in primary (trauma) care 2. To provide better quality rehabilitation services to SCI clients through building a appropriately designed 12 bed SCI ward block at GPHRC 3. To reduce the incidence of infections and pressure sores in SCI patients 
after their return home and thereby reduce the rate of re-admission and improve their quality of life

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: Physical Education Development Project in Nepal
rows.description:
The Physical Education Development Pilot Project 2013-2014 is LiiKe's first project in Nepal. It is planned in close cooperation with the Tribhuvan University's (TU) Department of Health Physical and Population Education which is the local partner in the p
roject. The pilot project is the first step to aiming at: - increasing the equal access to Physical Education for boys and girls - increasing the gender balance within the teachers who are teaching Physical Education- improving the quality of the PE educat
ion- getting the Physical Education into practice and outside the classroom when possible- raising awareness of the importance of Physical Education in the sense of physical health and fitness on the one hand and mental and social health of the students on
 the other hand- raising awareness of the positive impact that physical activity has on learning- raising awareness that through sports and games all the students can learn skills for life and sometimes find a career or become an athlete- improving the Phy
sical Education curriculum and- improving and formalizing the status of Physical Education as a school subject.In the project ten districts and twenty higher secondary schools (grades 11-12) are chosen from all of the five development regions of Nepal. Dis
tricts will be selected considering the Hill and Terai settings. Twenty teachers (female and male) get an intensive training for conducting games and sports for the students. Back in the schools and districts presentation will be given about the key findin
gs to community and the school presonnel. In the schools teachers start programme that includes different sports and games as regular activity and in the form of tournaments. The project schools are provided with necessary games and sports equipment. The s
tudents from grades 11 to 12 are the future primary school (grades 1-8) teachers which adds the value of the programme.The field supervisors are nominated to monitor and support the implementation of the programme in the schools. The experiences from the s
tudents and teachers will be collected before and after the school year in which the project is implemented and best practices manual 'How to make Physical Education accessible for boys and girls' will be made based on that. In the beginning of the project
 and after the implementation seminar will be held for all the stakeholders including the Ministry of Education in order to reach the decision-makers.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: NP/FLC NGO Federation of Nepal Promoting NGOs' Governance and Management in Nepal
rows.description:
The project aims to contribute for creating democratic and credible governance and management system in NGOs/CSOs of Nepal that could be scaled up to Nepali politics and society. The overall objective of the project is to increase access and voices of peop
le to basic services and entitlements as the rights-holders by expanding the roles of NGOs/CSOs in democratic governance and development which ultimately contribute for sustainable peace and conflict transformation in the country. The project aims to devel
op the capacity of NGOs/CSOs for embedding the key elements of institutional good governance which will enhance effectiveness of NGOs/CSOs in development process of Nepal. It will give a greater credibility to them for the development process they facilita
te. The specific objective of the project is to improve governance and management of NGOs/CSOs for effective delivery of basic services in the country by enhancing their legitimacy transparency accountability and integrity.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: NP/PYM Sarvodaya Nepal Teach for Nepal
rows.description:
The overall objective of the project is to  create national movement Teach for Nepal of young people to enhance quality of education in public schools in Nepal.  The project aims to attract outstanding university graduates and young professionals to teach 
in public and community schools for two years as Fellows primarily in maths science and English to decrease achievement gaps that exists between private and public schools students. The project will be co-funded with Nepalese private sector companies. Priv
ate sector will also offer working placements for the Fellows after their two years engagement is public schools.

rows.reporting: Ministry for Foreign Affairs of Finland
rows.title: EU/Mobilizing Youth for Gender Equality in Politics
rows.description:
Discrimination and unequal share of power are considered the major bottleneck for democracy in Nepal. Discrimination has restricted women and members of other marginalized groups from equal access to the state?s policy making positions. The proposed Action
 supports building up the pluralistic and representative democracy in Nepal by supporting cross-party forums for dialogue promote the use of non-violent activities for transformation and increase women's capacity to engage in political processes. The actio
n is based on notion that Women's meaningful participation in political processes reinforces sustainable peace and reduces the risk of politically initiated violence and halt in democratic processes.The Joint Youth and Student Platform in Nepal with suppor
t of DEMO Finland has experience and proven strengths of challenging the status quo of political party organisations as well as together tackle difficult cultural matters in society. This project will strengthen the Platform as a cross-party dialogue centr
e which has the will and the know-how to promote inclusion pluralism and equal participation in political parties.The duration of the project is 29 months in 2013-2015 and the proposed funding for the project is 37 500 euros which will be used during the f
irst year of the project .
